changes.detail: All five mandatory front matter fields present: work_id (CONTEXT-RUNTIME-1_reusable_runtime_context_evaluation_layer), branch (work/CONTEXT-RUNTIME-1-reusable-runtime-context-evaluation-layer), risk_level (HIGH), execution_model (TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH), change_type (MIXED). No field missing or malformed.

HIGH is correct and mandatory. Sprint proposes backend/core/analytics/runtime_context_evaluator.py (new Intelligence Core component) and integration into SignalEvaluator.evaluate_all() — a direct Intelligence Core method. Any work touching Intelligence Core = HIGH by default (SOP §10). MIXED is correct: new governed YAML (runtime_context_requirements_model_v1.yaml = CONTENT) consumed by a new Intelligence Core component (BEHAVIOUR). MIXED is governed by BEHAVIOUR controls per SOP §4. No reclassification warranted.

TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH is correct. Phase 1 = runtime capability audit and design report (no code changes). Phase 2 = implement reusable context evaluator, package metadata, governance model, tests. Phase 3 = activation decision gated by mandatory human STOP (READY_FOR_HUMAN_STOP_GATE + APPROVE BATCH2 CONTEXT GATED ACTIVATION). Structurally sound and appropriate for HIGH/Intelligence Core/MIXED work.

evaluate_all() signature confirmed: (self, signal_biomarkers: Dict[str, float], signal_derived: Dict[str, float], lab_ranges: Dict[str, dict], reference_profiles: Optional[Dict[str, dict]] = None). Zero context, questionnaire, sex, age, medication, or supplement parameters. Confirmed the capability gap. The mandatory_pre_emission_gates mechanism (lines 381-421) supports biomarker_present and numeric comparator_type only — no demographic or questionnaire gate type. This is the primary integration surface the sprint must extend.

signal_id: signal_free_t3_low (line 9). activation_logic: lab_range_exceeded (line 47). activation_config.enable_lower_bound: false (line 50). The signal cannot fire for low FT3. No mandatory_pre_emission_gates block present. Supporting metrics TSH and FT4 declared but not gated. Current state = cannot activate via any path. Sprint must add enable_lower_bound: true AND mandatory_pre_emission_gates for TSH + FT4 presence before activation is safe.

changes.finding: CONFIRMED — UPPER-BOUND ACTIVE, NO CONTEXT GATE
changes.detail: signal_id: signal_dhea_high. activation_logic: lab_range_exceeded. enable_upper_bound default=true (not explicitly false). Meaning: dhea_high would fire on lab-range upper-bound WITHOUT any sex, age, or medication context check if the package were activated. This confirms context gating must exist in the evaluator before any androgen package activation can be safe — the signal_library metadata alone is insufficient protection.

AnalysisContext model exists with: questionnaire_responses: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] (line 20), lifestyle_factors: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] (line 24), medical_history: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] (line 28). Sex (biological_sex), age (date_of_birth), medications (long_term_medications), supplements, and known conditions are all collectible via questionnaire_responses. The data exists upstream but is NOT threaded through to SignalEvaluator.evaluate_all(). The sprint's Phase 1 audit must trace the call path from AnalysisContext to evaluate_all() to identify the correct integration point.

CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 (line 77): Status=Open. Explicitly identifies all catalogue modifiers runtime_active=false as blocking androgen (8 packages) and FT3 low (illness/medication + TSH+FT4). Prerequisite for any remaining Batch 2 activation. This sprint resolves CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 if successful. CF-BATCH2-010 (line 76): Status=Open. Androgen clinical sign-off STILL REQUIRED after CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3. Sprint correctly does not close CF-BATCH2-010.

runtime_context_requirements_model_v1.yaml is a new file — no existing file with this name. context_modifier_catalogue_draft_v1.yaml is the single source of modifier governance, not a duplicate. The new execution register (context_runtime_execution_register_v1.yaml) is a sprint record, not an authority source. No duplicate authority source risk introduced.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 235-255 (required runtime behaviour), lines 354-369 (do-not-change list)
changes.detail: Intelligence Core surface area: (1) runtime_context_evaluator.py — new helper for context presence checks only; (2) small SignalEvaluator integration — context evaluation must be injected as a pre-emission gate, not by modifying threshold/override/state evaluation logic; (3) package signal_library metadata additions for FT3 low / androgen packages if governed. Evaluator must not invent unavailable context, must return structured missing-context result, must fail closed. Allowed changes precisely listed in prompt lines 344-354. Forbidden surfaces explicitly listed: clinical wording, thresholds, reference ranges, signal IDs, activation keys, frontend, SSOT, scoring, report compiler.

9 regression tests required: (1) signal with required demographic context does not emit when context missing; (2) signal with required companion biomarker does not emit when companion missing; (3) signal with required medication/supplement context does not emit when absent; (4) signal emits when all required context present; (5) unrelated signals without context requirements unaffected; (6) missing context fails closed; (7) FT3 low remains inactive unless TSH+FT4+illness/medication context satisfied; (8) androgen packages remain inactive unless required context satisfied; (9) no hardcoded medical thresholds introduced. All 9 are verifiable against source behaviour confirmed in this hardening.

Four determinism risks Cursor must handle: (1) Context evaluator must be pure-function — same input must always produce same gate result; (2) Fail-closed means absent context key returns suppress, never default-allow — must not infer or default missing context (prompt line 253); (3) Filtered output must maintain sort order (signal_id, activation_key) matching evaluate_all() sort at lines 531-534; (4) Package metadata additions must use package-declared requirements only — not bespoke marker-specific code (prompt line 549). No silent output drift allowed.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 354-369 (do not change list); lines 259-305 (metadata scope restriction)
changes.detail: Explicit do-not-change list: clinical wording, thresholds, reference ranges, signal IDs, activation keys, frontend, SSOT, scoring, report compiler, unrelated package logic. Package metadata scope restricted to FT3 low and androgen packages only if governed by prior medical review/binding. Prompt explicitly states: do not activate androgen packages merely because metadata exists (line 303). eGFR and other packages out of scope. No scope creep risk in prompt structure.

changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 101-110 (baseline STOP), lines 327-334 (Phase 1 STOP), lines 627-642 (general STOP conditions)
changes.detail: Baseline STOP covers: wrong branch, main drift, dirty tree, missing context_modifier_catalogue, missing batch2 blockers register, missing androgen package folders, missing FT3 package. Phase 1 STOP covers: no safe runtime location for context evaluation, runtime context shape cannot be determined, broad pipeline redesign required, unavailable context prevents safe activation. General STOP covers: validators fail, architecture gate fails, rollback undefined, hardcoded thresholds required. Coverage is comprehensive.

MANDATORY: CF-BATCH2-010 (androgen clinical sign-off) is OPEN and is separate from CF-CONTEXT-MOD-3 (runtime capability). Even if the runtime context layer is fully built and context gates are enforced, androgen packages cannot activate until clinical sign-off artefact exists in the repo. The batch2 blockers register records 4 androgen packages as KEEP_BLOCKED_MEDICAL_SIGNOFF_REQUIRED (dhea_high, free_testosterone_high at lines 58-70), not only KEEP_BLOCKED_RUNTIME_CAPABILITY_MISSING. Phase 3 expected outcome (androgen packages remain inactive) is correct. Context capability must be built as infrastructure; activation is a separate gate.

changes.detail: Rollback path is definable: delete runtime_context_evaluator.py, revert signal_evaluator.py integration (if any), revert signal_library.yaml mandatory_pre_emission_gates additions, delete runtime_context_requirements_model_v1.yaml, delete context_runtime_execution_register_v1.yaml, revert carry-forward register updates. All changes are file-local and reversible. No DB migration, no schema change, no irreversible operation.

All shell commands use python backend/scripts/... and git invocations in PowerShell syntax. No bash-specific syntax detected. Validation scripts are Python — compatible with Windows PowerShell environment confirmed for this repo.

hardening_notes: Sprint is hardened. Key findings for Cursor: (1) SignalEvaluator.evaluate_all() has zero context parameters — the integration point for runtime context evaluation must be identified by tracing AnalysisContext (backend/core/models/context.py line 12) through the pipeline to evaluate_all(). (2) FT3 low signal_library has enable_lower_bound: false — must be changed to true and mandatory_pre_emission_gates added for TSH+FT4 presence before FT3 low activation is safe. (3) Androgen packages will require both runtime context gates AND clinical sign-off (CF-BATCH2-010 still open) before activation — Phase 3 expected outcome is remain inactive. (4) All authority paths verified against source content with line citations. No STOP condition triggered. No architectural violations found. Sprint may proceed to Stage 3 (python backend/scripts/run_work_package.py start).
